rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARBARA ANN STEWART
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/21/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) Item 3g(3) of the petition should be checked, as there was a prior personal representative. (5) Items 5a(3) or (4) and 5a(7) or 8 of the petition must be answered. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/7/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HELEN NICKLAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/30/2025. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) The ending balance of cash assets in accounts must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. The statements or letters must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period. (LRSF 14.34.D). 2) No proposed order has been received or reviewed by the court (LRSF 14.7.A). 3) In a verified supplement to the petition, please clarify whether the property located at 2610-14 McAllister Street is an asset of the estate. 4) Please provide authority for distributions to petitioner and Sofia Nicklas absent an order for preliminary distribution from this court (Probate Code Sections 11620 and 11623((a)(1)). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later June 16, 2025, to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNA WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/21/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Four of the decedent's siblings survived her. Who are/were their successors-in-interest? Did they all die intestate? Did any have a trust? (2) The estate must be divided into five shares for each of the decedent's siblings who survived (his/her trustee or personal representative of his/her estate or his/her issue, pursuant to Probate Code section 13100) and the issue of the decedent's predeceased sibling. [Probate Code Section 11801, et seq., and LRSF 14.35 G.] (3) This court does not permit an initial accounting to exceed two years. [LRSF 14.34 A.] Furthermore, the accounting purportedly for the period beginning with the date of death, 12/30/07, reports interest and dividends received only in 2024. What happened to the interest and dividends between 2008 through 2023? An amendment to the petition must be filed to address these issues. Notice of the amendment must be given after the amendment is filed. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of the amendment and proof of service, as well as courtesy copies of any original 13100 affidavits that are conventionally filed, if applicable, to the assigned examiner by 7/7/25. If the amendment cannot be filed in time to give 15 days' notice before 7/21/25, Counsel should contact the assigned examiner to request additional time. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THEODORE PAUL JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/30/2025. The DE-121 form filed on 05/14/2025 listed additional recipients of notice as cousins of the decedent, but items 6 & 8 of the amended petition has not been supplemented to disclose any surviving issue of the decedent's grandparents. In addition, the following issues have not been addressed since the 01/13/2025 hearing: 1) Please provide facts in support of the allegation at item 7b(3) of the amended petition that the decedent's predeceased spouse had no interest in the estate real property listed at item 3d(4). 2) Re the attorney declaration filed on 12/16/2024: i) Did each uncle of the decedent have any issue? ii) Re "unknown sibling of Thomas Watson Jones", did this person predecease? iii) No family tree has been provided for the decedent's predeceased spouse. The family tree must show the predeceased spouse's relatives who would have been the decedent's heirs under Probate Code section 6402(e) or (g). Note: Even if it turned out that the predeceased spouse's relatives were not the decedent's heirs (because the decedent was actually survived by issue of grandparents), they are entitled to notice per Probate Code section 6402.5(a), absent proof that the predeceased spouse had no interest in the estate real property. 3) Did the decedent handwrite the month (May or 5) to date the will? Only the day (13) and year (20) handwritten above the decedent's signature are legible. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/07/2025. Notes emailed to counsel on 05/28/2025.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY SANDEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/30/2025 to be calendared with competing petition filed by Arthur Anthony. The following notes were sent via US mail (as no e-mail address or fax number was provided) to petitioner in pro per on 05/19/2025: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. A pleading cover sheet must be attached to this form. 4) No proof of publication is on file. 5) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. Note: If the decedent was a citizen of Sierra Leone, notice must be given (by mailing a copy of the DE-121 form with the remote appearance information) to the Embassy of Sierra Leone in the United States. See Probate Code section 8113. 6) Re item 3e(2) of petition: No bond waivers (DE-142 forms) are on file. 7) Re item 5a of petition: i) (1) or (2) (at least one box) must be checked. ii) (3) or (4) (at least one box) must be checked. iii) (7) or (8) (at least one box) must be checked. 8) Re item 8 of petition: i) Each listed person's relationship to the decedent must be specified. ii) If the decedent was survived by children, each child must be listed. iii) Petitioner (including an address) must be listed.
